heading: Humanitarian assistance code of conduct
label: 2370b Humanitarian assistance code of conduct
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Emergency Supplemental Appropriations Act for Defense, the Global War on Terror, and Tsunami Relief, 2005, and not as part of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 which comprises this chapter.

Assignment of Functions Relating to the Report to the Congress on Implementation of Humanitarian Assistance Code of ConductMemorandum of President of the United States, , 71 F.R. 30549, provided:May 12, 2006Memorandum for the Secretary of StatePursuant to the authority vested in me by the Constitution and laws of the United States, including , United States Code, the functions of the President under section 2110(c) of the Emergency Supplemental Appropriations Act for Defense, the Global War on Terror, and Tsunami Relief, 2005 (title II of ) (), are assigned to the Secretary of State.section 301 of title 3Public Law 109–1322 U.S.C. 2370bYou are authorized and directed to publish this memorandum in the Federal Register.George W. Bush.

Bush.

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: None of the funds made available for foreign operations, export financing, and related programs under the headings “Migration and Refugee Assistance”, “United States Emergency Refugee and Migration Assistance Fund”, “International Disaster and Famine Assistance”, or “Transition Initiatives” may be obligated to an organization that fails to adopt a code of conduct that provides for the protection of beneficiaries of assistance under any such heading from sexual exploitation and abuse in humanitarian relief operations.

children.heading: Consistency with United Nations principles
children.label: b Consistency with United Nations principles
children.text: The code of conduct referred to in subsection (a) shall, to the maximum extent practicable, be consistent with the six core principles of the United Nations Inter-Agency Standing Committee Task Force on Protection From Sexual Exploitation and Abuse in Humanitarian Crises.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: c Reports
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , and not later than one year after , the President shall transmit to the appropriate congressional committees a report on the implementation of this section.May 11, 2005May 11, 2005

children.heading: Effective Date
children.label: d Effective Date
children.text: This section shall take effect 60 days after , and shall apply to funds obligated after such date for fiscal year 2005 and any subsequent fiscal year.May 11, 2005
